1.text: 起初，神创造天地。
1.text_en: In the beginning<WH7225> God<WH430> created<WH1254><WH853> the heaven<WH8064> and<WH853> the earth<WH776>.

1.text: 地是空虚混沌，渊面黑暗；神的灵运行在水面上。
1.text_en: And the earth<WH776> was<WH1961> without form<WH8414>, and void<WH922>; and darkness<WH2822> <FI>was<Fi> upon the face<WH6440> of the deep<WH8415>. And the Spirit<WH7307> of God<WH430> moved<WH7363> upon<WH5921> the face<WH6440> of the waters<WH4325>.<CM>

1.text: 神说：“要有光”，就有了光。
1.text_en: And God<WH430> said<WH559>, Let there be<WH1961> light<WH216>: and there was light<WH216>.

1.text: 神看光是好的，就把光暗分开了。
1.text_en: And God<WH430> saw<WH7200><WH853> the light<WH216>, that<WH3588> <FI>it was<Fi> good<WH2896>: and God<WH430> divided<WH914><WH996> the light<WH216> from<WH996> the darkness<WH2822>.<RF>the light from…: Heb. between the light and between the darkness<Rf>

1.text: 神称光为“昼”，称暗为“夜”。有晚上，有早晨，这是头一日。
1.text_en: And God<WH430> called<WH7121> the light<WH216> Day<WH3117>, and the darkness<WH2822> he called<WH7121> Night<WH3915>. And the evening<WH6153> and the morning<WH1242> were the first<WH259> day<WH3117>.<RF>And the evening…: Heb. And the evening was, and the morning was<Rf><CM>

1.text: 神说：“诸水之间要有空气，将水分为上下。”
1.text_en: And God<WH430> said<WH559>, Let there be a firmament<WH7549> in the midst<WH8432> of the waters<WH4325>, and let it divide<WH914> the waters<WH4325> from the waters<WH4325>.<RF>firmament: Heb. expansion<Rf>

1.text: 神就造出空气，将空气以下的水、空气以上的水分开了。事就这样成了。
1.text_en: And God<WH430> made<WH6213> the firmament<WH7549>, and divided<WH914> the waters<WH4325> which<WH834> <FI>were<Fi> under<WH8478> the firmament<WH7549> from the waters<WH4325> which<WH834> <FI>were<Fi> above<WH5921> the firmament<WH7549>: and it was so<WH3651>.

1.text: 神称空气为“天”。有晚上，有早晨，是第二日。
1.text_en: And God<WH430> called<WH7121> the firmament<WH7549> Heaven<WH8064>. And the evening<WH6153> and the morning<WH1242> were the second<WH8145> day<WH3117>.<CM>

1.text: 神说：“天下的水要聚在一处，使旱地露出来。”事就这样成了。
1.text_en: And God<WH430> said<WH559>, Let the waters<WH4325> under the heaven<WH8064> be gathered together<WH6960> unto<WH413> one<WH259> place<WH4725>, and let the dry<WH3004> <FI>land<Fi> appear<WH7200>: and it was so.

1.text: 神称旱地为“地”，称水的聚处为“海”。神看着是好的。
1.text_en: And God<WH430> called<WH7121> the dry<WH3004> <FI>land<Fi> Earth<WH776>; and the gathering together<WH4723> of the waters<WH4325> called<WH7121> he Seas<WH3220>: and God<WH430> saw<WH7200> that <FI>it was<Fi> good<WH2896>.

1.text: 神说：“地要发生青草和结种子的菜蔬，并结果子的树木，各从其类，果子都包着核。”事就这样成了。
1.text_en: And God<WH430> said<WH559>, Let the earth<WH776> bring forth<WH1876> grass<WH1877>, the herb<WH6212> yielding<WH2232> seed<WH2233>, <FI>and<Fi> the fruit<WH6529> tree<WH6086> yielding<WH6213> fruit<WH6529> after his kind<WH4327>, whose<WH834> seed<WH2233> <FI>is<Fi> in itself, upon the earth<WH776>: and it was so.<RF>grass: Heb. tender grass<Rf>

1.text: 于是地发生了青草和结种子的菜蔬，各从其类；并结果子的树木，各从其类；果子都包着核。神看着是好的。
1.text_en: And the earth<WH776> brought forth<WH3318> grass<WH1877>, <FI>and<Fi> herb<WH6212> yielding<WH2232> seed<WH2233> after his kind<WH4327>, and the tree<WH6086> yielding<WH6213> fruit<WH6529>, whose seed<WH2233> <FI>was<Fi> in itself, after his kind<WH4327>: and God<WH430> saw<WH7200> that <FI>it was<Fi> good<WH2896>.

1.text: 有晚上，有早晨，是第三日。
1.text_en: And the evening<WH6153> and the morning<WH1242> were the third<WH7992> day<WH3117>.<CM>

1.text: 神说：“天上要有光体，可以分昼夜，作记号，定节令、日子、年岁，
1.text_en: And God<WH430> said<WH559>, Let there be lights<WH3974> in the firmament<WH7549> of the heaven<WH8064> to divide<WH914> the day<WH3117> from the night<WH3915>; and let them be for signs<WH226>, and for seasons<WH4150>, and for days<WH3117>, and years<WH8141>:<RF>the day…: Heb. between the day and between the night<Rf>

1.text: 并要发光在天空，普照在地上。”事就这样成了。
1.text_en: And let them be for lights<WH3974> in the firmament<WH7549> of the heaven<WH8064> to give light<WH215> upon the earth<WH776>: and it was so.

1.text: 于是神造了两个大光，大的管昼，小的管夜，又造众星，
1.text_en: And God<WH430> made<WH6213> two<WH8147> great<WH1419> lights<WH3974>; the greater<WH1419> light<WH3974> to rule<WH4475> the day<WH3117>, and the lesser<WH6996> light<WH3974> to rule<WH4475> the night<WH3915>: <FI>he made<Fi> the stars<WH3556> also.<RF>to rule the day…: Heb. for the rule of the day, etc.<Rf>

1.text: 就把这些光摆列在天空，普照在地上，
1.text_en: And God<WH430> set<WH5414> them in the firmament<WH7549> of the heaven<WH8064> to give light<WH215> upon the earth<WH776>,

1.text: 管理昼夜，分别明暗。神看着是好的。
1.text_en: And to rule<WH4910> over the day<WH3117> and over the night<WH3915>, and to divide<WH914> the light<WH216> from the darkness<WH2822>: and God<WH430> saw<WH7200> that <FI>it was<Fi> good<WH2896>.

1.text: 有晚上，有早晨，是第四日。
1.text_en: And the evening<WH6153> and the morning<WH1242> were the fourth<WH7243> day<WH3117>.<CM>

1.text: 神说：“水要多多滋生有生命的物，要有雀鸟飞在地面以上，天空之中。”
1.text_en: And God<WH430> said<WH559>, Let the waters<WH4325> bring forth abundantly<WH8317> the moving creature<WH8318> that hath life<WH2416><WH5315>, and fowl<WH5775> <FI>that<Fi> may fly<WH5774> above<WH5921> the earth<WH776> in the open<WH6440> firmament<WH7549> of heaven<WH8064>.<RF>moving: or, creeping<Rf><RF>life: Heb. soul<Rf><RF>fowl…: Heb. let fowl fly<Rf><RF>open…: Heb. face of the firmament of heaven<Rf>

1.text: 神就造出大鱼和水中所滋生各样有生命的动物，各从其类；又造出各样飞鸟，各从其类。神看着是好的。
1.text_en: And God<WH430> created<WH1254> great<WH1419> whales<WH8577>, and every living<WH2416> creature<WH5315> that moveth<WH7430>, which the waters<WH4325> brought forth abundantly<WH8317>, after their kind<WH4327>, and every winged<WH3671> fowl<WH5775> after his kind<WH4327>: and God<WH430> saw<WH7200> that <FI>it was<Fi> good<WH2896>.

1.text: 神就赐福给这一切，说：“滋生繁多，充满海中的水；雀鸟也要多生在地上。”
1.text_en: And God<WH430> blessed<WH1288> them, saying<WH559>, Be fruitful<WH6509>, and multiply<WH7235>, and fill<WH4390> the waters<WH4325> in the seas<WH3220>, and let fowl<WH5775> multiply<WH7235> in the earth<WH776>.

1.text: 有晚上，有早晨，是第五日。
1.text_en: And the evening<WH6153> and the morning<WH1242> were the fifth<WH2549> day<WH3117>.<CM>

1.text: 神说：“地要生出活物来，各从其类；牲畜、昆虫、野兽，各从其类。”事就这样成了。
1.text_en: And God<WH430> said<WH559>, Let the earth<WH776> bring forth<WH3318> the living<WH2416> creature<WH5315> after his kind<WH4327>, cattle<WH929>, and creeping thing<WH7431>, and beast<WH2416> of the earth<WH776> after his kind<WH4327>: and it was so.

1.text: 于是神造出野兽，各从其类；牲畜，各从其类；地上一切昆虫，各从其类。神看着是好的。
1.text_en: And God<WH430> made<WH6213> the beast<WH2416> of the earth<WH776> after his kind<WH4327>, and cattle<WH929> after their kind<WH4327>, and every thing that creepeth<WH7431> upon the earth<WH127> after his kind<WH4327>: and God<WH430> saw<WH7200> that <FI>it was<Fi> good<WH2896>.<CM>

1.text: 神说：“我们要照着我们的形像，按着我们的样式造人，使他们管理海里的鱼、空中的鸟、地上的牲畜和全地，并地上所爬的一切昆虫。”
1.text_en: And God<WH430> said<WH559>, Let us make<WH6213> man<WH120> in our image<WH6754>, after our likeness<WH1823>: and let them have dominion<WH7287> over the fish<WH1710> of the sea<WH3220>, and over the fowl<WH5775> of the air<WH8064>, and over the cattle<WH929>, and over all the earth<WH776>, and over every creeping thing<WH7431> that creepeth<WH7430> upon the earth<WH776>.

1.text: 神就照着自己的形像造人，乃是照着他的形像造男造女。
1.text_en: So God<WH430> created<WH1254> man<WH120> in his <FI>own<Fi> image<WH6754>, in the image<WH6754> of God<WH430> created<WH1254> he him; male<WH2145> and female<WH5347> created<WH1254> he them.

1.text: 神就赐福给他们，又对他们说：“要生养众多，遍满地面，治理这地；也要管理海里的鱼、空中的鸟，和地上各样行动的活物。”
1.text_en: And God<WH430> blessed<WH1288> them, and God<WH430> said<WH559> unto them, Be fruitful<WH6509>, and multiply<WH7235>, and replenish<WH4390> the earth<WH776>, and subdue it<WH3533>: and have dominion<WH7287> over the fish<WH1710> of the sea<WH3220>, and over the fowl<WH5775> of the air<WH8064>, and over every living thing<WH2416> that moveth<WH7430> upon the earth<WH776>.<RF>moveth: Heb. creepeth<Rf><CM>

1.text: 神说：“看哪，我将遍地上一切结种子的菜蔬和一切树上所结有核的果子，全赐给你们作食物。
1.text_en: And God<WH430> said<WH559>, Behold<WH2009>, I have given<WH5414> you every herb<WH6212> bearing<WH2232> seed<WH2233>, which <FI>is<Fi> upon the face<WH6440> of all the earth<WH776>, and every tree<WH6086>, in the which <FI>is<Fi> the fruit<WH6529> of a tree<WH6086> yielding<WH2232> seed<WH2233>; to you it shall be<WH1961> for meat<WH402>.<RF>bearing…: Heb. seeding seed<Rf><RF>yielding…: Heb. seeding seed<Rf>

1.text: 至于地上的走兽和空中的飞鸟，并各样爬在地上有生命的物，我将青草赐给它们作食物。”事就这样成了。
1.text_en: And to every beast<WH2416> of the earth<WH776>, and to every fowl<WH5775> of the air<WH8064>, and to every thing that creepeth<WH7430> upon the earth<WH776>, wherein <FI>there is<Fi> life<WH2416><WH5315>, <FI>I have given<Fi> every green<WH3418> herb<WH6212> for meat<WH402>: and it was so.<RF>life: Heb. a living soul<Rf><CM>

1.text: 神看着一切所造的都甚好。有晚上，有早晨，是第六日。
1.text_en: And God<WH430> saw<WH7200> every thing that<WH834> he had made<WH6213>, and, behold, <FI>it was<Fi> very<WH3966> good<WH2896>. And the evening<WH6153> and the morning<WH1242> were the sixth<WH8345> day<WH3117>.<CM>

2.text: 天地万物都造齐了。
2.text_en: Thus the heavens<WH8064> and the earth<WH776> were finished<WH3615>, and all the host of them<WH6635>.

2.text: 到第七日，神造物的工已经完毕，就在第七日歇了他一切的工，安息了。
2.text_en: And on the seventh<WH7637> day<WH3117> God<WH430> ended<WH3615> his work<WH4399> which he had made<WH6213>; and he rested<WH7673> on the seventh<WH7637> day<WH3117> from all his work<WH4399> which he had made<WH6213>.

2.text: 神赐福给第七日，定为圣日，因为在这日神歇了他一切创造的工，就安息了。
2.text_en: And God<WH430> blessed<WH1288> the seventh<WH7637> day<WH3117>, and sanctified<WH6942> it: because<WH3588> that in it he had rested<WH7673> from all his work<WH4399> which God<WH430> created<WH1254> and made<WH6213>.<RF>created…: Heb. created to make<Rf><CM>

2.text: 创造天地的来历，在耶和华神造天地的日子，乃是这样：
2.text_en: These<WH428> <FI>are<Fi> the generations<WH8435> of the heavens<WH8064> and of the earth<WH776> when they were created<WH1254>, in the day<WH3117> that the LORD<WH3068> God<WH430> made<WH6213> the earth<WH776> and the heavens<WH8064>,

2.text: 野地还没有草木，田间的菜蔬还没有长起来，因为耶和华神还没有降雨在地上，也没有人耕地，
2.text_en: And every plant<WH7880> of the field<WH7704> before<WH2962> it was in the earth<WH776>, and every herb<WH6212> of the field<WH7704> before<WH2962> it grew<WH6779>: for<WH3588> the LORD<WH3068> God<WH430> had not<WH3808> caused it to rain<WH4305> upon the earth<WH776>, and <FI>there was<Fi> not<WH369> a man<WH120> to till<WH5647> the ground<WH127>.

2.text: 但有雾气从地上腾，滋润遍地。
2.text_en: But there went up<WH5927> a mist<WH108> from<WH4480> the earth<WH776>, and watered<WH8248> the whole face<WH6440> of the ground<WH127>.<RF>there…: or, a mist which went up from, etc.<Rf>

2.text: 耶和华神用地上的尘土造人，将生气吹在他鼻孔里，他就成了有灵的活人，名叫亚当。
2.text_en: And the LORD<WH3068> God<WH430> formed<WH3335> man<WH120> <FI>of<Fi> the dust<WH6083> of<WH4480> the ground<WH127>, and breathed<WH5301> into his nostrils<WH639> the breath<WH5397> of life<WH2416>; and man<WH120> became a living<WH2416> soul<WH5315>.<RF>of the dust…: Heb. dust of the ground<Rf><CM>

2.text: 耶和华神在东方的伊甸立了一个园子，把所造的人安置在那里。
2.text_en: And the LORD<WH3068> God<WH430> planted<WH5193> a garden<WH1588> eastward<WH6924> in Eden<WH5731>; and there<WH8033> he put<WH7760> the man<WH120> whom<WH834> he had formed<WH3335>.

2.text: 耶和华神使各样的树从地里长出来，可以悦人的眼目，其上的果子好作食物。园子当中又有生命树和分别善恶的树。
2.text_en: And out<WH4480> of the ground<WH127> made<WH6779> the LORD<WH3068> God<WH430> to grow<WH6779> every tree<WH6086> that is pleasant<WH2530> to the sight<WH4758>, and good<WH2896> for food<WH3978>; the tree<WH6086> of life<WH2416> also in the midst<WH8432> of the garden<WH1588>, and the tree<WH6086> of knowledge<WH1847> of good<WH2896> and evil<WH7451>.

2.text: 有河从伊甸流出来滋润那园子，从那里分为四道：
2.text_en: And a river<WH5104> went out<WH3318> of Eden<WH5731> to water<WH8248> the garden<WH1588>; and from thence it was parted<WH6504>, and became into four<WH702> heads<WH7218>.

2.text: 第一道名叫比逊，就是环绕哈腓拉全地的。在那里有金子，
2.text_en: The name<WH8034> of the first<WH259> <FI>is<Fi> Pison<WH6376>: that<WH1931> <FI>is<Fi> it which compasseth<WH5437> the whole land<WH776> of Havilah<WH2341>, where<WH834> <FI>there is<Fi> gold<WH2091>;

2.text: 并且那地的金子是好的；在那里又有珍珠和红玛瑙。
2.text_en: And the gold<WH2091> of that<WH1931> land<WH776> <FI>is<Fi> good<WH2896>: there <FI>is<Fi> bdellium<WH916> and the onyx<WH7718> stone<WH68>.

2.text: 第二道河名叫基训，就是环绕古实全地的。
2.text_en: And the name<WH8034> of the second<WH8145> river<WH5104> <FI>is<Fi> Gihon<WH1521>: the same <FI>is<Fi> it that compasseth<WH5437> the whole land<WH776> of Ethiopia<WH3568>.<RF>Ethiopia: Heb. Cush<Rf>

2.text: 第三道河名叫底格里斯，流在亚述的东边。第四道河就是幼发拉底河。
2.text_en: And the name<WH8034> of the third<WH7992> river<WH5104> <FI>is<Fi> Hiddekel<WH2313>: that <FI>is<Fi> it which goeth toward<WH1980> the east<WH6926> of Assyria<WH804>. And the fourth<WH7243> river<WH5104> <FI>is<Fi> Euphrates<WH6578>.<RF>toward…: or, eastward to Assyria<Rf>

2.text: 耶和华神将那人安置在伊甸园，使他修理看守。
2.text_en: And the LORD<WH3068> God<WH430> took<WH3947> the man<WH120>, and put him<WH3240> into the garden<WH1588> of Eden<WH5731> to dress<WH5647> it and to keep<WH8104> it.<RF>the man: or, Adam<Rf><CM>

2.text: 耶和华神吩咐他说：“园中各样树上的果子，你可以随意吃；
2.text_en: And the LORD<WH3068> God<WH430> commanded<WH6680> the man<WH120>, saying<WH559>, Of every tree<WH6086> of the garden<WH1588> thou mayest freely<WH398> eat<WH398>:<RF>thou…: Heb. eating thou shalt eat<Rf>

2.text: 只是分别善恶树上的果子，你不可吃，因为你吃的日子必定死。”
2.text_en: But of the tree<WH6086> of the knowledge<WH1847> of good<WH2896> and evil<WH7451>, thou shalt not eat<WH398> of it: for in the day<WH3117> that thou eatest<WH398> thereof thou shalt surely<WH4191> die<WH4191>.<RF>thou shalt surely…: Heb. dying thou shalt die<Rf><CM>

2.text: 耶和华神说：“那人独居不好，我要为他造一个配偶帮助他。”
2.text_en: And the LORD<WH3068> God<WH430> said<WH559>, <FI>It is<Fi> not good<WH2896> that the man<WH120> should be<WH1961> alone; I will make<WH6213> him an help meet<WH5828> for him.<RF>meet…: Heb. as before him<Rf>

2.text: 耶和华神用土所造成的野地各样走兽和空中各样飞鸟都带到那人面前，看他叫什么。那人怎样叫各样的活物，那就是它的名字。
2.text_en: And out of the ground<WH127> the LORD<WH3068> God<WH430> formed<WH3335> every beast<WH2416> of the field<WH7704>, and every fowl<WH5775> of the air<WH8064>; and brought<WH935> <FI>them<Fi> unto Adam<WH120> to see<WH7200> what he would call<WH7121> them: and whatsoever Adam<WH120> called<WH7121> every living<WH2416> creature<WH5315>, that<WH1931> <FI>was<Fi> the name thereof<WH8034>.<RF>Adam: or, the man<Rf>

2.text: 那人便给一切牲畜和空中飞鸟、野地走兽都起了名；只是那人没有遇见配偶帮助他。
2.text_en: And Adam<WH120> gave<WH7121> names<WH8034> to all cattle<WH929>, and to the fowl<WH5775> of the air<WH8064>, and to every beast<WH2416> of the field<WH7704>; but for Adam<WH120> there was not found<WH4672> an help meet<WH5828> for him.<RF>gave: Heb. called<Rf><CM>

2.text: 耶和华神使他沉睡，他就睡了；于是取下他的一条肋骨，又把肉合起来。
2.text_en: And the LORD<WH3068> God<WH430> caused<WH5307> a deep sleep<WH8639> to fall<WH5307> upon Adam<WH121>, and he slept<WH3462>: and he took<WH3947> one<WH259> of his ribs<WH6763>, and closed up<WH5462> the flesh<WH1320> instead thereof;

2.text: 耶和华神就用那人身上所取的肋骨造成一个女人，领她到那人跟前。
2.text_en: And the rib<WH6763>, which the LORD<WH3068> God<WH430> had taken<WH3947> from man<WH120>, made<WH1129> he a woman<WH802>, and brought<WH935> her unto the man<WH120>.<RF>made: Heb. builded<Rf>

2.text: 那人说：“这是我骨中的骨，肉中的肉，可以称她为‘女人’，因为她是从男人身上取出来的。”
2.text_en: And Adam<WH120> said<WH559>, This<WH2063> <FI>is<Fi> now<WH6471> bone<WH6106> of my bones<WH6106>, and flesh<WH1320> of my flesh<WH1320>: she<WH2063> shall be called<WH7121> Woman<WH802>, because she<WH2063> was taken<WH3947> out of Man<WH376>.<RF>Woman: Heb. Isha<Rf><RF>Man: Heb. Ish<Rf>

2.text: 因此，人要离开父母，与妻子连合，二人成为一体。
2.text_en: Therefore<WH3651> shall a man<WH376> leave<WH5800> his father<WH1> and his mother<WH517>, and shall cleave<WH1692> unto his wife<WH802>: and they shall be one<WH259> flesh<WH1320>.

2.text: 当时夫妻二人赤身露体，并不羞耻。
2.text_en: And they were both<WH8147> naked<WH6174>, the man<WH120> and his wife<WH802>, and were not ashamed<WH954>.<CM>

3.text: 耶和华神所造的，惟有蛇比田野一切的活物更狡猾。蛇对女人说：“神岂是真说不许你们吃园中所有树上的果子吗？”
3.text_en: Now the serpent<WH5175> was<WH1961> more subtil<WH6175> than any beast<WH2416> of the field<WH7704> which the LORD<WH3068> God<WH430> had made<WH6213>. And he said<WH559> unto the woman<WH802>, Yea<WH637>, hath God<WH430> said<WH559>, Ye shall not eat<WH398> of every tree<WH6086> of the garden<WH1588>?<RF>Yea…: Heb. Yea, because, etc.<Rf>

3.text: 女人对蛇说：“园中树上的果子，我们可以吃；
3.text_en: And the woman<WH802> said<WH559> unto the serpent<WH5175>, We may eat<WH398> of the fruit<WH6529> of the trees<WH6086> of the garden<WH1588>:

3.text: 惟有园当中那棵树上的果子，神曾说：‘你们不可吃，也不可摸，免得你们死。’”
3.text_en: But of the fruit<WH6529> of the tree<WH6086> which <FI>is<Fi> in the midst<WH8432> of the garden<WH1588>, God<WH430> hath said<WH559>, Ye shall not eat<WH398> of it, neither shall ye touch<WH5060> it, lest<WH6435> ye die<WH4191>.

3.text: 蛇对女人说：“你们不一定死，
3.text_en: And the serpent<WH5175> said<WH559> unto the woman<WH802>, Ye shall not surely<WH4191> die<WH4191>:

3.text: 因为神知道，你们吃的日子眼睛就明亮了，你们便如神能知道善恶。”
3.text_en: For God<WH430> doth know<WH3045> that in the day<WH3117> ye eat<WH398> thereof, then your eyes<WH5869> shall be opened<WH6491>, and ye shall be as gods<WH430>, knowing<WH3045> good<WH2896> and evil<WH7451>.<CM>

3.text: 于是女人见那棵树的果子好作食物，也悦人的眼目，且是可喜爱的，能使人有智慧，就摘下果子来吃了；又给她丈夫，她丈夫也吃了。
3.text_en: And when the woman<WH802> saw<WH7200> that the tree<WH6086> <FI>was<Fi> good<WH2896> for food<WH3978>, and that it<WH1931> <FI>was<Fi> pleasant<WH8378> to the eyes<WH5869>, and a tree<WH6086> to be desired<WH2530> to make <FI>one<Fi> wise<WH7919>, she took<WH3947> of the fruit thereof<WH6529>, and did eat<WH398>, and gave<WH5414> also<WH1571> unto her husband<WH376> with her; and he did eat<WH398>.<RF>pleasant: Heb. a desire<Rf>

3.text: 他们二人的眼睛就明亮了，才知道自己是赤身露体，便拿无花果树的叶子，为自己编作裙子。
3.text_en: And the eyes<WH5869> of them both<WH8147> were opened<WH6491>, and they<WH1992> knew<WH3045> that they <FI>were<Fi> naked<WH5903>; and they sewed<WH8609> fig<WH8384> leaves<WH5929> together<WH8609>, and made themselves<WH6213> aprons<WH2290>.<RF>aprons: or, things to gird about<Rf>

3.text: 天起了凉风，耶和华神在园中行走。那人和他妻子听见神的声音，就藏在园里的树木中，躲避耶和华神的面。
3.text_en: And they heard<WH8085> the voice<WH6963> of the LORD<WH3068> God<WH430> walking<WH1980> in the garden<WH1588> in the cool<WH7307> of the day<WH3117>: and Adam<WH120> and his wife<WH802> hid themselves<WH2244> from the presence<WH6440> of the LORD<WH3068> God<WH430> amongst<WH8432> the trees<WH6086> of the garden<WH1588>.<RF>cool: Heb. wind<Rf><CM>

3.text: 耶和华神呼唤那人，对他说：“你在哪里？”
3.text_en: And the LORD<WH3068> God<WH430> called<WH7121> unto Adam<WH120>, and said<WH559> unto him, Where <FI>art<Fi> thou<WH335>?

3.text: 他说：“我在园中听见你的声音，我就害怕，因为我赤身露体，我便藏了。”
3.text_en: And he said<WH559>, I heard<WH8085> thy voice<WH6963> in the garden<WH1588>, and I was afraid<WH3372>, because I<WH595> <FI>was<Fi> naked<WH5903>; and I hid myself<WH2244>.<CM>

3.text: 耶和华说：“谁告诉你赤身露体呢？莫非你吃了我吩咐你不可吃的那树上的果子吗？”
3.text_en: And he said<WH559>, Who<WH4310> told<WH5046> thee that thou <FI>wast<Fi> naked<WH5903>? Hast thou eaten<WH398> of the tree<WH6086>, whereof I commanded thee<WH6680> that thou shouldest not<WH1115> eat<WH398>?

3.text: 那人说：“你所赐给我、与我同居的女人，她把那树上的果子给我，我就吃了。”
3.text_en: And the man<WH120> said<WH559>, The woman<WH802> whom thou gavest<WH5414> <FI>to be<Fi> with me<WH5978>, she<WH1931> gave<WH5414> me of the tree<WH6086>, and I did eat<WH398>.

3.text: 耶和华神对女人说：“你作的是什么事呢？”女人说：“那蛇引诱我，我就吃了。”
3.text_en: And the LORD<WH3068> God<WH430> said<WH559> unto the woman<WH802>, What <FI>is<Fi> this <FI>that<Fi> thou hast done<WH6213>? And the woman<WH802> said<WH559>, The serpent<WH5175> beguiled me<WH5377>, and I did eat<WH398>.<CM>

3.text: 耶和华神对蛇说：“你既作了这事，就必受咒诅，比一切的牲畜野兽更甚；你必用肚子行走，终身吃土。
3.text_en: And the LORD<WH3068> God<WH430> said<WH559> unto the serpent<WH5175>, Because thou<WH859> hast done<WH6213> this, thou <FI>art<Fi> cursed<WH779> above all cattle<WH929>, and above every beast<WH2416> of the field<WH7704>; upon thy belly<WH1512> shalt thou go<WH3212>, and dust<WH6083> shalt thou eat<WH398> all the days<WH3117> of thy life<WH2416>:

3.text: 我又要叫你和女人彼此为仇；你的后裔和女人的后裔也彼此为仇。女人的后裔要伤你的头，你要伤他的脚跟。”
3.text_en: And I will put<WH7896> enmity<WH342> between thee and the woman<WH802>, and between thy seed<WH2233> and her seed<WH2233>; it shall bruise<WH7779> thy head<WH7218>, and thou shalt bruise<WH7779> his heel<WH6119>.<CM>

3.text: 又对女人说：“我必多多加增你怀胎的苦楚，你生产儿女必多受苦楚。你必恋慕你丈夫，你丈夫必管辖你。”
3.text_en: Unto the woman<WH802> he said<WH559>, I will greatly<WH7235> multiply<WH7235> thy sorrow<WH6093> and thy conception<WH2032>; in sorrow<WH6089> thou shalt bring forth<WH3205> children<WH1121>; and thy desire<WH8669> <FI>shall be<Fi> to thy husband<WH376>, and he shall rule<WH4910> over thee.<RF>to thy…: or, subject to thy husband<Rf><CM>

3.text: 又对亚当说：“你既听从妻子的话，吃了我所吩咐你不可吃的那树上的果子，地必为你的缘故受咒诅。你必终身劳苦，才能从地里得吃的。
3.text_en: And unto Adam<WH121> he said<WH559>, Because thou hast hearkened<WH8085> unto the voice<WH6963> of thy wife<WH802>, and hast eaten<WH398> of the tree<WH6086>, of which<WH834> I commanded thee<WH6680>, saying<WH559>, Thou shalt not eat<WH398> of it: cursed<WH779> <FI>is<Fi> the ground<WH127> for thy sake; in sorrow<WH6093> shalt thou eat<WH398> <FI>of<Fi> it all<WH3605> the days<WH3117> of thy life<WH2416>;

3.text: 地必给你长出荆棘和蒺藜来，你也要吃田间的菜蔬。
3.text_en: Thorns also<WH6975> and thistles<WH1863> shall it bring forth<WH6779> to thee; and thou shalt eat<WH398> the herb<WH6212> of the field<WH7704>;<RF>bring…: Heb. cause to bud<Rf>

3.text: 你必汗流满面才得糊口，直到你归了土，因为你是从土而出的。你本是尘土，仍要归于尘土。”
3.text_en: In the sweat<WH2188> of thy face<WH639> shalt thou eat<WH398> bread<WH3899>, till<WH5704> thou return<WH7725> unto the ground<WH127>; for out of it wast thou taken<WH3947>: for dust<WH6083> thou<WH859> <FI>art<Fi>, and unto dust<WH6083> shalt thou return<WH7725>.<CM>

3.text: 亚当给他妻子起名叫夏娃，因为她是众生之母。
3.text_en: And Adam<WH120> called<WH7121> his wife's<WH802> name<WH8034> Eve<WH2332>; because she was the mother<WH517> of all living<WH2416>.<RF>Eve: Heb. Chavah: that is Living<Rf><CM>

3.text: 耶和华神为亚当和他妻子用皮子作衣服给他们穿。
3.text_en: Unto Adam<WH120> also and to his wife<WH802> did the LORD<WH3068> God<WH430> make<WH6213> coats<WH3801> of skins<WH5785>, and clothed them<WH3847>.<CM>

3.text: 耶和华神说：“那人已经与我们相似，能知道善恶。现在恐怕他伸手又摘生命树的果子吃，就永远活着。”
3.text_en: And the LORD<WH3068> God<WH430> said<WH559>, Behold<WH2005>, the man<WH120> is become as one<WH259> of us, to know<WH3045> good<WH2896> and evil<WH7451>: and now, lest he put forth<WH7971> his hand<WH3027>, and take<WH3947> also of the tree<WH6086> of life<WH2416>, and eat<WH398>, and live<WH2425> for ever<WH5769>:

3.text: 耶和华神便打发他出伊甸园去，耕种他所自出之土。
3.text_en: Therefore the LORD<WH3068> God<WH430> sent him forth<WH7971> from the garden<WH1588> of Eden<WH5731>, to till<WH5647> the ground<WH127> from whence he was taken<WH3947>.

3.text: 于是把他赶出去了；又在伊甸园的东边安设基路伯，和四面转动发火焰的剑，要把守生命树的道路。
3.text_en: So he drove out<WH1644> the man<WH120>; and he placed<WH7931> at the east<WH6924> of the garden<WH1588> of Eden<WH5731> Cherubims<WH3742>, and a flaming<WH3858> sword<WH2719> which turned every way<WH2015>, to keep<WH8104> the way<WH1870> of the tree<WH6086> of life<WH2416>.<CM>

1.text: 约瑟和他的弟兄们，并那一代的人都死了。
1.text_en: Now these <FI>are<Fi> the names<WH8034> of the children<WH1121> of Israel<WH3478>, which came<WH935> into Egypt<WH4714>; every man<WH376> and his household<WH1004> came<WH935> with Jacob<WH3290>.

1.text: 以色列人生养众多，并且繁茂，极其强盛，满了那地。
1.text_en: Reuben<WH7205>, Simeon<WH8095>, Levi<WH3878>, and Judah<WH3063>,

1.text: 有不认识约瑟的新王起来治理埃及，
1.text_en: Issachar<WH3485>, Zebulun<WH2074>, and Benjamin<WH1144>,

1.text: 对他的百姓说：“看哪，这以色列民比我们还多，又比我们强盛。
1.text_en: Dan<WH1835>, and Naphtali<WH5321>, Gad<WH1410>, and Asher<WH836>.

1.text: 来吧，我们不如用巧计待他们，恐怕他们多起来，日后若遇什么争战的事，就连合我们的仇敌攻击我们，离开这地去了。”
1.text_en: And all the souls<WH5315> that came out<WH3318> of the loins<WH3409> of Jacob<WH3290> were seventy<WH7657> souls<WH5315>: for Joseph<WH3130> was in Egypt<WH4714> <FI>already<Fi>.<RF>loins: Heb. thigh<Rf>

1.text: 于是埃及人派督工的辖制他们，加重担苦害他们。他们为法老建造两座积货城，就是比东和兰塞。
1.text_en: And Joseph<WH3130> died<WH4191>, and all his brethren<WH251>, and all that generation<WH1755>.

1.text: 只是越发苦害他们，他们越发多起来，越发蔓延；埃及人就因以色列人愁烦。
1.text_en: And the children<WH1121> of Israel<WH3478> were fruitful<WH6509>, and increased abundantly<WH8317>, and multiplied<WH7235>, and waxed exceeding<WH3966><WH3966> mighty<WH6105>; and the land<WH776> was filled<WH4390> with them.<CM>

1.text: 埃及人严严地使以色列人作工，
1.text_en: Now there arose up<WH6965> a new<WH2319> king<WH4428> over Egypt<WH4714>, which knew<WH3045> not Joseph<WH3130>.

1.text: 使他们因作苦工觉得命苦；无论是和泥，是作砖，是作田间各样的工，在一切的工上都严严地待他们。
1.text_en: And he said<WH559> unto his people<WH5971>, Behold, the people<WH5971> of the children<WH1121> of Israel<WH3478> <FI>are<Fi> more<WH7227> and mightier<WH6099> than we:

1.text: 法老吩咐他的众民说：“以色列人所生的男孩，你们都要丢在河里；一切的女孩，你们要存留她的性命。”
1.text_en: Come on<WH3051>, let us deal wisely<WH2449> with them; lest they multiply<WH7235>, and it come to pass, that, when there falleth out<WH7122> any war<WH4421>, they<WH1931> join<WH3254> also unto our enemies<WH8130>, and fight<WH3898> against us, and <FI>so<Fi> get them up<WH5927> out of the land<WH776>.

2.text: 有一个利未家的人，娶了一个利未女子为妻。
2.text_en: And there went<WH3212> a man<WH376> of the house<WH1004> of Levi<WH3878>, and took<WH3947> <FI>to wife<Fi> a daughter<WH1323> of Levi<WH3878>.

2.text: 那女人怀孕，生一个儿子，见他俊美，就藏了他三个月。
2.text_en: And the woman<WH802> conceived<WH2029>, and bare<WH3205> a son<WH1121>: and when she saw<WH7200> him that he <FI>was a<Fi> goodly<WH2896> <FI>child<Fi>, she hid<WH6845> him three<WH7969> months<WH3391>.

2.text: 后来不能再藏，就取了一个蒲草箱，抹上石漆和石油，将孩子放在里头，把箱子搁在河边的芦荻中。
2.text_en: And when she could<WH3201> not longer<WH5750> hide<WH6845> him, she took<WH3947> for him an ark<WH8392> of bulrushes<WH1573>, and daubed<WH2560> it with slime<WH2564> and with pitch<WH2203>, and put<WH7760> the child<WH3206> therein; and she laid<WH7760> <FI>it<Fi> in the flags<WH5488> by the river's<WH2975> brink<WH8193>.

2.text: 孩子的姐姐远远站着，要知道他究竟怎么样。
2.text_en: And his sister<WH269> stood<WH3320> afar off<WH7350>, to wit<WH3045> what would be done<WH6213> to him.<CM>

2.text: 法老的女儿来到河边洗澡，她的使女们在河边行走。她看见箱子在芦荻中，就打发一个婢女拿来。
2.text_en: And the daughter<WH1323> of Pharaoh<WH6547> came down<WH3381> to wash<WH7364> <FI>herself<Fi> at the river<WH2975>; and her maidens<WH5291> walked<WH1980> along by the river's<WH2975> side<WH3027>; and when she saw<WH7200> the ark<WH8392> among<WH8432> the flags<WH5488>, she sent<WH7971> her maid<WH519> to fetch<WH3947> it.

2.text: 她打开箱子，看见那孩子。孩子哭了，她就可怜他，说：“这是希伯来人的一个孩子。”
2.text_en: And when she had opened<WH6605> <FI>it<Fi>, she saw<WH7200> the child<WH3206>: and, behold, the babe<WH5288> wept<WH1058>. And she had compassion<WH2550> on him, and said<WH559>, This <FI>is one<Fi> of the Hebrews'<WH5680> children<WH3206>.

2.text: 孩子的姐姐对法老的女儿说：“我去在希伯来妇人中叫一个奶妈来，为你奶这孩子，可以不可以？”
2.text_en: Then said<WH559> his sister<WH269> to Pharaoh's<WH6547> daughter<WH1323>, Shall I go<WH3212> and call<WH7121> to thee a nurse<WH3243> of the Hebrew<WH5680> women<WH802>, that she may nurse<WH3243> the child<WH3206> for thee?

2.text: 法老的女儿说：“可以。”童女就去叫了孩子的母亲来。
2.text_en: And Pharaoh's<WH6547> daughter<WH1323> said<WH559> to her, Go<WH3212>. And the maid<WH5959> went<WH3212> and called<WH7121> the child's<WH3206> mother<WH517>.

2.text: 法老的女儿对她说：“你把这孩子抱去，为我奶他，我必给你工价。”妇人就抱了孩子去奶他。
2.text_en: And Pharaoh's<WH6547> daughter<WH1323> said<WH559> unto her, Take<WH3212> this child<WH3206> away<WH3212>, and nurse<WH3243> it for me, and I will give<WH5414> <FI>thee<Fi> thy wages<WH7939>. And the woman<WH802> took<WH3947> the child<WH3206>, and nursed<WH5134> it.

2.text: 孩子渐长，妇人把他带到法老的女儿那里，就作了她的儿子。她给孩子起名叫摩西，意思说：“因我把他从水里拉出来。”
2.text_en: And the child<WH3206> grew<WH1431>, and she brought<WH935> him unto Pharaoh's<WH6547> daughter<WH1323>, and he became her son<WH1121>. And she called<WH7121> his name<WH8034> Moses<WH4872>: and she said<WH559>, Because I drew<WH4871> him out of the water<WH4325>.<RF>Moses: that is, Drawn out<Rf><CM>

2.text: 后来摩西长大，他出去到他弟兄那里，看他们的重担，见一个埃及人打希伯来人的一个弟兄。
2.text_en: And it came to pass in those days<WH3117>, when Moses<WH4872> was grown<WH1431>, that he went out<WH3318> unto his brethren<WH251>, and looked<WH7200> on their burdens<WH5450>: and he spied<WH7200> an Egyptian<WH4713><WH376> smiting<WH5221> an Hebrew<WH5680>, one of his brethren<WH251>.

2.text: 他左右观看，见没有人，就把埃及人打死了，藏在沙土里。
2.text_en: And he looked<WH6437> this way<WH3541> and that way<WH3541>, and when he saw<WH7200> that <FI>there was<Fi> no man<WH376>, he slew<WH5221> the Egyptian<WH4713>, and hid<WH2934> him in the sand<WH2344>.

2.text: 第二天他出去，见有两个希伯来人争斗，就对那欺负人的说：“你为什么打你同族的人呢？”
2.text_en: And when he went out<WH3318> the second<WH8145> day<WH3117>, behold, two<WH8147> men<WH582> of the Hebrews<WH5680> strove<WH5327> together: and he said<WH559> to him that did the wrong<WH7563>, Wherefore smitest<WH5221> thou thy fellow<WH7453>?

2.text: 那人说：“谁立你作我们的首领和审判官呢？难道你要杀我，像杀那埃及人吗？”摩西便害怕，说：“这事必是被人知道了。”
2.text_en: And he said<WH559>, Who made<WH7760> thee<WH376> a prince<WH8269> and a judge<WH8199> over us? intendest<WH559> thou to kill<WH2026> me, as thou killedst<WH2026> the Egyptian<WH4713>? And Moses<WH4872> feared<WH3372>, and said<WH559>, Surely<WH403> this thing<WH1697> is known<WH3045>.<RF>a prince: Heb. a man, a prince<Rf>

2.text: 法老听见这事，就想杀摩西，但摩西躲避法老，逃往米甸地居住。
2.text_en: Now when Pharaoh<WH6547> heard<WH8085> this thing<WH1697>, he sought<WH1245> to slay<WH2026> Moses<WH4872>. But Moses<WH4872> fled<WH1272> from the face<WH6440> of Pharaoh<WH6547>, and dwelt<WH3427> in the land<WH776> of Midian<WH4080>: and he sat down<WH3427> by a well<WH875>.<CM>

2.text: 一日，他在井旁坐下。米甸的祭司有七个女儿，她们来打水，打满了槽，要饮父亲的群羊。
2.text_en: Now the priest<WH3548> of Midian<WH4080> had seven<WH7651> daughters<WH1323>: and they came<WH935> and drew<WH1802> <FI>water<Fi>, and filled<WH4390> the troughs<WH7298> to water<WH8248> their father's<WH1> flock<WH6629>.<RF>priest: or, prince<Rf>

2.text: 有牧羊的人来把她们赶走了，摩西就起来帮助她们，又饮了她们的群羊。
2.text_en: And the shepherds<WH7462> came<WH935> and drove them away<WH1644>: but Moses<WH4872> stood up<WH6965> and helped<WH3467> them, and watered<WH8248> their flock<WH6629>.

2.text: 她们来到父亲流珥那里，他说：“今日你们为何来得这么快呢？”
2.text_en: And when they came<WH935> to Reuel<WH7467> their father<WH1>, he said<WH559>, How<WH4069> <FI>is it that<Fi> ye are come<WH935> so soon<WH4116> to day<WH3117>?<RF>Reuel: called also Jethro, or, Jether<Rf>

2.text: 她们说：“有一个埃及人救我们脱离牧羊人的手，并且为我们打水饮了群羊。”
2.text_en: And they said<WH559>, An Egyptian<WH4713><WH376> delivered<WH5337> us out of the hand<WH3027> of the shepherds<WH7462>, and also drew<WH1802> <FI>water<Fi> enough<WH1802> for us, and watered<WH8248> the flock<WH6629>.

2.text: 他对女儿们说：“那个人在哪里？你们为什么撇下他呢？你们去请他来吃饭。”
2.text_en: And he said<WH559> unto his daughters<WH1323>, And where <FI>is<Fi> he? why <FI>is<Fi> it <FI>that<Fi> ye have left<WH5800> the man<WH376>? call<WH7121> him, that he may eat<WH398> bread<WH3899>.

2.text: 摩西甘心和那人同住；那人把他的女儿西坡拉给摩西为妻。
2.text_en: And Moses<WH4872> was content<WH2974> to dwell<WH3427> with the man<WH376>: and he gave<WH5414> Moses<WH4872> Zipporah<WH6855> his daughter<WH1323>.

2.text: 西坡拉生了一个儿子，摩西给他起名叫革舜，意思说：“因我在外邦作了寄居的。”
2.text_en: And she bare<WH3205> <FI>him<Fi> a son<WH1121>, and he called<WH7121> his name<WH8034> Gershom<WH1647>: for he said<WH559>, I have been a stranger<WH1616> in a strange<WH5237> land<WH776>.<RF>Gershom: that is, A stranger here<Rf><CM>

3.text: 摩西牧养他岳父米甸祭司流珥的羊群；一日，领羊群往野外去，到了神的山，就是何烈山。
3.text_en: Now Moses<WH4872> kept<WH1961><WH7462> the flock<WH6629> of Jethro<WH3503> his father in law<WH2859>, the priest<WH3548> of Midian<WH4080>: and he led<WH5090> the flock<WH6629> to the backside<WH310> of the desert<WH4057>, and came<WH935> to the mountain<WH2022> of God<WH430>, <FI>even<Fi> to Horeb<WH2722>.

3.text: 耶和华的使者从荆棘里火焰中向摩西显现。摩西观看，不料，荆棘被火烧着，却没有烧毁。
3.text_en: And the angel<WH4397> of the LORD<WH3068> appeared<WH7200> unto him in a flame<WH3827> of fire<WH784> out of the midst<WH8432> of a bush<WH5572>: and he looked<WH7200>, and, behold, the bush<WH5572> burned<WH1197> with fire<WH784>, and the bush<WH5572> <FI>was<Fi> not consumed<WH398>.

3.text: 摩西说：“我要过去看这大异象，这荆棘为何没有烧坏呢？”
3.text_en: And Moses<WH4872> said<WH559>, I will now turn aside<WH5493>, and see<WH7200> this great<WH1419> sight<WH4758>, why<WH4069> the bush<WH5572> is not burnt<WH1197>.

3.text: 耶和华神见他过去要看，就从荆棘里呼叫说：“摩西！摩西！”他说：“我在这里。”
3.text_en: And when the LORD<WH3068> saw<WH7200> that he turned aside<WH5493> to see<WH7200>, God<WH430> called<WH7121> unto him out of the midst<WH8432> of the bush<WH5572>, and said<WH559>, Moses<WH4872>, Moses<WH4872>. And he said<WH559>, Here <FI>am<Fi> I.

3.text: 神说：“不要近前来。当把你脚上的鞋脱下来，因为你所站之地是圣地。”
3.text_en: And he said<WH559>, Draw not nigh<WH7126> hither<WH1988>: put off<WH5394> thy shoes<WH5275> from off thy feet<WH7272>, for the place<WH4725> whereon<WH834> thou standest<WH5975> <FI>is<Fi> holy<WH6944> ground<WH127>.

3.text: 又说：“我是你父亲的神，是亚伯拉罕的神，以撒的神，雅各的神。”摩西蒙上脸，因为怕看神。
3.text_en: Moreover he said<WH559>, I <FI>am<Fi> the God<WH430> of thy father<WH1>, the God<WH430> of Abraham<WH85>, the God<WH430> of Isaac<WH3327>, and the God<WH430> of Jacob<WH3290>. And Moses<WH4872> hid<WH5641> his face<WH6440>; for he was afraid<WH3372> to look<WH5027> upon God<WH430>.<CM>

3.text: 耶和华说：“我实在看见我百姓在埃及所受的困苦，
3.text_en: And the LORD<WH3068> said<WH559>, I have surely<WH7200> seen<WH7200> the affliction<WH6040> of my people<WH5971> which <FI>are<Fi> in Egypt<WH4714>, and have heard<WH8085> their cry<WH6818> by reason<WH6440> of their taskmasters<WH5065>; for I know<WH3045> their sorrows<WH4341>;

3.text: 也听见他们因受督工的辖制所发的哀声，我原知道他们的痛苦。
3.text_en: And I am come down<WH3381> to deliver<WH5337> them out of the hand<WH3027> of the Egyptians<WH4714>, and to bring<WH5927> them up out of that land<WH776> unto a good<WH2896> land<WH776> and a large<WH7342>, unto a land<WH776> flowing<WH2100> with milk<WH2461> and honey<WH1706>; unto the place<WH4725> of the Canaanites<WH3669>, and the Hittites<WH2850>, and the Amorites<WH567>, and the Perizzites<WH6522>, and the Hivites<WH2340>, and the Jebusites<WH2983>.

3.text: 我下来是要救他们脱离埃及人的手，领他们出了那地，到美好宽阔流奶与蜜之地，就是到迦南人、赫人、亚摩利人、比利洗人、希未人、耶布斯人之地。
3.text_en: Now therefore, behold, the cry<WH6818> of the children<WH1121> of Israel<WH3478> is come<WH935> unto me: and I have also seen<WH7200> the oppression<WH3906> wherewith the Egyptians<WH4714> oppress<WH3905> them.

3.text: 现在以色列人的哀声达到我耳中，我也看见埃及人怎样欺压他们。
3.text_en: Come<WH3212> now therefore, and I will send<WH7971> thee unto Pharaoh<WH6547>, that thou mayest bring forth<WH3318> my people<WH5971> the children<WH1121> of Israel<WH3478> out of Egypt<WH4714>.<CM>

3.text: 所以我打发你去，使你可以将我的百姓以色列人从埃及领出来。”
3.text_en: And Moses<WH4872> said<WH559> unto God<WH430>, Who <FI>am<Fi> I, that I should go<WH3212> unto Pharaoh<WH6547>, and that I should bring forth<WH3318> the children<WH1121> of Israel<WH3478> out of Egypt<WH4714>?

3.text: 摩西对神说：“我是什么人，竟能去见法老，将以色列人从埃及领出来呢？”
3.text_en: And he said<WH559>, Certainly I will be with thee; and this <FI>shall be<Fi> a token<WH226> unto thee, that<WH3588> I have sent<WH7971> thee: When thou hast brought forth<WH3318> the people<WH5971> out of Egypt<WH4714>, ye shall serve<WH5647> God<WH430> upon this mountain<WH2022>.

3.text: 神说：“我必与你同在。你将百姓从埃及领出来之后，你们必在这山上事奉我，这就是我打发你去的证据。”
3.text_en: And Moses<WH4872> said<WH559> unto God<WH430>, Behold, <FI>when<Fi> I come<WH935> unto the children<WH1121> of Israel<WH3478>, and shall say<WH559> unto them, The God<WH430> of your fathers<WH1> hath sent<WH7971> me unto you; and they shall say<WH559> to me, What <FI>is<Fi> his name<WH8034>? what shall I say<WH559> unto them?

3.text: 摩西对神说：“我到以色列人那里，对他们说：‘你们祖宗的神打发我到你们这里来。’他们若问我说：‘他叫什么名字？’我要对他们说什么呢？”
3.text_en: And God<WH430> said<WH559> unto Moses<WH4872>, I AM<WH1961> THAT I AM<WH1961>: and he said<WH559>, Thus shalt thou say<WH559> unto the children<WH1121> of Israel<WH3478>, I AM hath sent<WH7971> me unto you.

3.text: 神对摩西说：“我是自有永有的。”又说：“你要对以色列人这样说：‘那自有的打发我到你们这里来。’”
3.text_en: And God<WH430> said<WH559> moreover<WH5750> unto Moses<WH4872>, Thus shalt thou say<WH559> unto the children<WH1121> of Israel<WH3478>, The LORD<WH3068> God<WH430> of your fathers<WH1>, the God<WH430> of Abraham<WH85>, the God<WH430> of Isaac<WH3327>, and the God<WH430> of Jacob<WH3290>, hath sent<WH7971> me unto you: this <FI>is<Fi> my name<WH8034> for ever<WH5769>, and this <FI>is<Fi> my memorial<WH2143> unto all<WH1755> generations<WH1755>.<CM>

3.text: 神又对摩西说：“你要对以色列人这样说：‘耶和华你们祖宗的神，就是亚伯拉罕的神，以撒的神，雅各的神，打发我到你们这里来。’耶和华是我的名，直到永远；这也是我的纪念，直到万代。
3.text_en: Go<WH3212>, and gather<WH622> the elders<WH2205> of Israel<WH3478> together<WH622>, and say<WH559> unto them, The LORD<WH3068> God<WH430> of your fathers<WH1>, the God<WH430> of Abraham<WH85>, of Isaac<WH3327>, and of Jacob<WH3290>, appeared<WH7200> unto me, saying<WH559>, I have surely<WH6485> visited<WH6485> you, and <FI>seen<Fi> that which is done<WH6213> to you in Egypt<WH4714>:

3.text: 你去招聚以色列的长老，对他们说：‘耶和华你们祖宗的神，就是亚伯拉罕的神，以撒的神，雅各的神，向我显现，说：我实在眷顾了你们，也看见你们在埃及所受的困苦。
3.text_en: And I have said<WH559>, I will bring<WH5927> you up out of the affliction<WH6040> of Egypt<WH4714> unto the land<WH776> of the Canaanites<WH3669>, and the Hittites<WH2850>, and the Amorites<WH567>, and the Perizzites<WH6522>, and the Hivites<WH2340>, and the Jebusites<WH2983>, unto a land<WH776> flowing<WH2100> with milk<WH2461> and honey<WH1706>.

3.text: 我也说要将你们从埃及的困苦中领出来，往迦南人、赫人、亚摩利人、比利洗人、希未人、耶布斯人的地去，就是到流奶与蜜之地。’
3.text_en: And they shall hearken<WH8085> to thy voice<WH6963>: and thou shalt come<WH935>, thou and the elders<WH2205> of Israel<WH3478>, unto the king<WH4428> of Egypt<WH4714>, and ye shall say<WH559> unto him, The LORD<WH3068> God<WH430> of the Hebrews<WH5680> hath met<WH7136> with us: and now let us go<WH3212>, we beseech thee, three<WH7969> days'<WH3117> journey<WH1870> into the wilderness<WH4057>, that we may sacrifice<WH2076> to the LORD<WH3068> our God<WH430>.

3.text: 他们必听你的话。你和以色列的长老要去见埃及王，对他说：‘耶和华希伯来人的神遇见了我们。现在求你容我们往旷野去，走三天的路程，为要祭祀耶和华我们的神。’
3.text_en: And I am sure<WH3045> that the king<WH4428> of Egypt<WH4714> will not let<WH5414> you go<WH1980>, no, not by a mighty<WH2389> hand<WH3027>.<RF>no…: or, but by strong hand<Rf>

3.text: 我料定埃及王必不容你们去，我必伸手在埃及中间施行我一切的奇事，攻击那地，然后他才容你们去。
3.text_en: And I will stretch out<WH7971> my hand<WH3027>, and smite<WH5221> Egypt<WH4714> with all my wonders<WH6381> which I will do<WH6213> in the midst<WH7130> thereof: and after<WH310> that he will let you go<WH7971>.

3.text: 我必叫你们在埃及人眼前蒙恩，你们去的时候，就不至于空手而去。
3.text_en: And I will give<WH5414> this people<WH5971> favour<WH2580> in the sight<WH5869> of the Egyptians<WH4714>: and it shall come to pass, that, when ye go<WH3212>, ye shall not go<WH3212> empty<WH7387>:

3.text: 但各妇女必向她的邻舍，并居住在她家里的女人，要金器银器和衣裳，好给你们的儿女穿戴。这样你们就把埃及人的财物夺去了。”
3.text_en: But every woman<WH802> shall borrow<WH7592> of her neighbour<WH7934>, and of her that sojourneth<WH1481> in her house<WH1004>, jewels<WH3627> of silver<WH3701>, and jewels<WH3627> of gold<WH2091>, and raiment<WH8071>: and ye shall put<WH7760> <FI>them<Fi> upon your sons<WH1121>, and upon your daughters<WH1323>; and ye shall spoil<WH5337> the Egyptians<WH4714>.<RF>the Egyptians: or, Egypt<Rf><CM>

1.text: 亚伯拉罕的后裔，大卫的子孙，耶稣基督的家谱：

1.text: 雅各生约瑟，就是马利亚的丈夫。那称为基督的耶稣，是从马利亚生的。
1.text_en: And<WG1161> Jacob<WG2384> begat<WG1080> Joseph<WG2501> the husband<WG435> of Mary<WG3137>, of<WG1537> whom<WG3739> was born<WG1080> Jesus<WG2424>, who<WG3588> is called<WG3004> Christ<WG5547>.

1.text: 这样，从亚伯拉罕到大卫，共有十四代；从大卫到迁至巴比伦的时候，也有十四代；从迁至巴比伦的时候到基督，又有十四代。
1.text_en: So<WG3767> all<WG3956> the generations<WG1074> from<WG575> Abraham<WG11> to<WG2193> David<WG1138> <FI>are<Fi> fourteen<WG1180> generations<WG1074>; and<WG2532> from<WG575> David<WG1138> until<WG2193> the carrying away<WG3350> into Babylon<WG897> <FI>are<Fi> fourteen<WG1180> generations<WG1074>; and<WG2532> from<WG575> the carrying away<WG3350> into Babylon<WG897> unto<WG2193> Christ<WG5547> <FI>are<Fi> fourteen<WG1180> generations<WG1074>.<CM>

1.text: 耶稣基督降生的事记在下面：他母亲马利亚已经许配给约瑟，还没有迎娶，马利亚就从圣灵怀了孕。
1.text_en: Now<WG1161> the birth<WG1083> of Jesus<WG2424> Christ<WG5547> was<WG2258> on this wise<WG3779>: When as<WG1063> his<WG846> mother<WG3384> Mary<WG3137> was espoused<WG3423> to Joseph<WG2501>, before<WG4250><WG2228> they<WG846> came together<WG4905>, she was found<WG2147> with child<WG1722><WG1064><WG2192> of<WG1537> the Holy<WG40> Ghost<WG4151>.

1.text: 她丈夫约瑟是个义人，不愿意明明地羞辱她，想要暗暗地把她休了。
1.text_en: Then<WG1161> Joseph<WG2501> her<WG846> husband<WG435>, being<WG5607> a just<WG1342> <FI>man<Fi>, and<WG2532> not<WG3361> willing<WG2309> to make<WG3856> her<WG846> a publick example<WG3856>, was minded<WG1014> to put<WG630> her<WG846> away<WG630> privily<WG2977>.

1.text: 正思念这事的时候，有主的使者向他梦中显现，说：“大卫的子孙约瑟，不要怕，只管娶过你的妻子马利亚来，因她所怀的孕是从圣灵来的。
1.text_en: But<WG1161> while he<WG846> thought on<WG1760> these things<WG5023>, behold<WG2400>, the angel<WG32> of the Lord<WG2962> appeared<WG5316> unto him<WG846> in<WG2596> a dream<WG3677>, saying<WG3004>, Joseph<WG2501>, thou son<WG5207> of David<WG1138>, fear<WG5399> not<WG3361> to take<WG3880> unto thee<WG4675> Mary<WG3137> thy wife<WG1135>: for<WG1063> that which is conceived<WG1080> in<WG1722> her<WG846> is<WG2076> of<WG1537> the Holy<WG40> Ghost<WG4151>.<RF>conceived: Gr. begotten<Rf>

1.text: 她将要生一个儿子，你要给他起名叫耶稣，因他要将自己的百姓从罪恶里救出来。”
